picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are on a historic 10-game playoff winning streak, with all wins coming by double digits, and have covered the spread in every road game this postseason. They have consistently beaten the Cleveland Cavaliers by an average of 13.3 points in the first three games of this series, far exceeding the -2.5 spread. Both teams are fully healthy, but New York's superior form and momentum make them a strong pick to cover this modest spread.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: New York leads the Eastern Conference Finals 3-0 and is one win away from the NBA Finals, riding a dominant 10-game postseason winning streak. The Cavaliers are facing elimination at home, a deficit no team in NBA history has overcome. With no significant injuries for either side, the Knicks' sustained high-level play and series control point to an outright victory.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The total line has seen upward movement, indicating sharp money on the Over. The Knicks have consistently put up high scores in this series (121, 109, 121), and the Cavaliers have also managed to score over 100 points in all three games despite their losses. The combined offensive efficiency of both teams, with the Knicks averaging 122.2 PPG in their last 10, and a model projection of 229.9 total points, indicates strong value for the Over.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Despite trailing 3-0 in the series, the Cavaliers are playing on their home court in a must-win elimination game, which often brings out a heightened level of desperation and effort. While the Knicks have been dominant, the market is still giving Cleveland a home-court bump, suggesting a tighter contest than the previous games. Donovan Mitchell and James Harden are healthy and capable of leading a strong offensive performance to keep the game competitive and potentially cover the spread.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: An elimination game often leads to a more defensive-minded approach, especially from the team fighting for its season, which could suppress scoring. The New York Knicks, despite their offensive efficiency, play at one of the slowest paces in the league (27th in NBA). The Cavaliers will likely attempt to slow the game down to limit New York's possessions and control the tempo, further contributing to a lower-scoring affair. This increased defensive intensity and slower pace could result in the total staying Under 218.5.

The Carolina Hurricanes enter this Game 3 with a dominant 9-1 postseason record and are unbeaten on the road in the playoffs. They boast a fully healthy roster, which is a significant advantage in the Eastern Conference Final. In contrast, the Montreal Canadiens are without key forward Patrik Laine due to an abdominal injury, impacting their offensive depth and power play. The Hurricanes demonstrated their resilience by winning Game 2 in overtime after a Game 1 loss, showcasing their ability to adjust and perform under pressure. This strong form and health advantage make Carolina a solid moneyline pick.

The total has seen significant line movement towards the Over 5.5, shifting from an opening of -117 to the current -130, indicating sharp money on a higher-scoring game. While Game 2 was a tighter 3-2 contest, Game 1 of this series exploded for 8 goals with Montreal winning 6-2. Montreal's power play can be potent, and Carolina's offense, averaging 2.9 goals per game in the playoffs, is capable of contributing significantly. With the series tied 1-1 and both teams eager to gain an edge, an offensive battle is a strong possibility.

The Montreal Canadiens have an impressive head-to-head trend against the Hurricanes, covering the +1.5 spread in all of their last five matchups. Even in their Game 2 overtime loss, they managed to cover this spread, demonstrating their ability to keep games close against Carolina. Playing at home in Game 3, the Canadiens have a perfect historical record in this specific playoff scenario when the series is tied 1-1, suggesting a strong performance is expected. Despite Patrik Laine's absence, Montreal's home-ice advantage and historical spread performance provide a strong edge for this pick.

Logan Stankoven has been a pivotal offensive force for the Carolina Hurricanes throughout the postseason, leading the team in both expected goals (5) and actual goals (7). He is confirmed active and plays a crucial role as the second-line center and on the second power-play unit, providing ample opportunities for point production. Given Carolina's strong offensive capabilities and Stankoven's consistent performance, getting plus-money odds for him to record at least one point presents excellent value. His consistent offensive output makes this a high-confidence prop bet.

The Carolina Hurricanes finished the regular season with a superior 53-22-7 record compared to Montreal's 48-24-10, highlighting their overall strength. Their 9-1 playoff record and unbeaten road performance in the postseason underscore their ability to dominate opponents. With Carolina's full roster health and Montreal missing key offensive player Patrik Laine, the Hurricanes possess a significant talent advantage. This combination of superior form, health, and road dominance provides a strong foundation for Carolina to win by more than one goal, offering excellent value at +176 odds.

The Athletics present strong value with a 27-26 record and a 2-1 lead in the season series against the Mariners. Their starting pitcher, Aaron Civale, boasts a 5-1 record and a 3.31 ERA, significantly outperforming Seattle's Luis Castillo (1-5, 6.41 ERA). Furthermore, Oakland's offense ranks 5th in wRC+ over the last 30 days, while Seattle is 7th, providing a clear offensive edge. The Athletics have also won three of their last five games, demonstrating better recent form than the Mariners.

The Padres, despite being home underdogs, hold a superior overall record of 31-21 compared to the Phillies' 26-27. San Diego also boasts better pitching metrics with a 3.84 team ERA and a 1.25 WHIP, against Philadelphia's 4.16 ERA and 1.33 WHIP. The pitching matchup favors San Diego's Randy Vasquez over an inconsistent Jesus Luzardo, and a significant 79.5% of the betting handle is on the Padres moneyline, indicating sharp money.

The Arizona Diamondbacks have dominated the head-to-head matchups this season, winning all three previous encounters with scores of 12-2, 5-3, and 6-3. Arizona enters this game in strong recent form, having won four of their last five games and currently on a two-game winning streak. Despite the Giants being favored, the Diamondbacks' more trustworthy offense and strong historical performance against San Francisco make the plus-money value highly attractive.

The Toronto Blue Jays are on a four-game winning streak, showing strong momentum as they return home. They have a significant pitching advantage with Trey Yesavage, who has been brilliant since returning from the injured list with a 1.07 ERA and no home runs allowed this season. In contrast, Miami's starter Janson Junk carries a 5.07 ERA. The Blue Jays' offense is also showing signs of waking up, further supporting a home victory.

The Dodgers are a dominant force with a 33-20 record and have won eight of their last ten games. The Rockies, conversely, are in last place at 20-34 and will start Tanner Gordon, who has a 6.59 ERA and a 1.50 WHIP, with his team holding an 0-7 record in his appearances. Los Angeles has also controlled the season series, winning three of four matchups, including decisive 12-3 and 7-1 victories. The significant pitching mismatch and the Dodgers' superior form make them a strong play on the run line.
